calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Confirming Validity Of Trust; Confirming Successor Trustee; And Confirming Asset To Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Sep-06-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Michael L. Dobrov for and with Sigfreda Shaposhnikov.

The Court stated before it today is a Petition for Order Confirming Validity of Trust; Confirming Successor Trustee; And Confirming Asset to Revocable Trust. Mr. Dobrov confirmed for the Court that he had received the probate examiner's notes but that he did not see the tentative ruling. The Court stated that there was a lot of information in the tentative ruling and that going forward that counsel is to check all locations for information. Mr. Dobrov presented argument in support of Michael being the sole beneficiary, stated that Michael survived his Sister Sofia, and that he takes because he met the requirement. Mr. Dobrov also stated that there were changes between the will, that there was talk of a codicil & adding Augusta, that he wasn't involved, that they went to another attorney in Oakland, and that Ernesto was added as a contingent beneficiary. The Court inquired about contact with Ernesto Scholochow. Ms. Shaposhnikov stated that Ernesto was in the hospital in Argentina, that he's out now, and that he is getting better. The Court inquired if anyone had spoken with Ernesto. Mr. Dobrov stated that they had sent him documents and that his Sister Augusta talked to him. Ms. Shaposhnikov stated that Ernesto is her cousin. The Court briefly voir dired Ms. Shaposhnikov. Ms. Shaposhnikov stated Ernesto went to the hospital for a heart issue, that she talked to Tatiana (Ernesto's daughter), and that she did not talk to Ernesto directly. The Court noted that there were no direct conversations with Ernesto and addressed the names of Ms. Hoffmark's parents. Ms. Shaposhnikov stated that they grew up in World War II, that it wasn't their legal names, that there were aliases, that they found information on the internet, that they looked up Sofia, that she got the names from the birth certificate, that they died long before, and that she had never met them. Mr. Dobrov addressed the trust, stating that they did not go through him, that they went through an attorney in Oakland (Kristin Pace), that the decedent lived in SF, that she was in poor health & couldn't travel, and that she wanted to avoid probate. Ms. Shaposhnikov stated that when her Aunt made the will she was in a hurry, that her Aunt didn't have children, that her Aunt planned on working out the details later, that Mr. Dobrov drafted something but it was confusing & complicated, that her Aunt wanted the change, and that Ernesto benefitted from "Iliana's" trust. At the conclusion of argument, the Court took the Petition for Order Confirming Validity of Trust; Confirming Successor Trustee; And Confirming Asset to Revocable Trust under submission. Mr. Dobrov submitted an order in open court.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.
